levels.title: Receipt Panic
levels.prompt: Flash the receipt, hide it, then rebuild the tiny disaster in order.

solution.explanation: Repeat the flashed receipt order, not the shuffled choice order.

failure: You filed the receipt by vibes. Accounting is unimpressed.
Three tiny tiles entered. Your confidence left with the wrong bag.
delay: TAP, DUR, ZIP. The receipt is short enough to fit in a sneeze.
scorecard: You rebuilt the receipt order. The buffer stopped making modem sounds.

levels.uqiq_moment: The first Pack 5 sequence looks like a receipt, but the choices arrive in reverse order.

levels.title: Queue Cutters
levels.prompt: Flash the queue, then put the cutters back where they were.

solution.explanation: The correct queue is the flashed order: RED, BLUE, OK.

failure: You let OK cut the line. Tiny clipboard chaos.
The queue had three spots and still became a parking problem.
delay: RED starts it. BLUE follows. OK gets to stop being dramatic.
scorecard: You restored the queue. The line stopped pretending it was a strategy.

levels.uqiq_moment: The word OK appears last in the flash but first in the choice row, baiting a lazy tap.

levels.title: Cache Spill
levels.prompt: The cache spills four tiles. Flash it, hide it, rebuild it.

solution.explanation: Four-item Memory Flash Levels still use the exact flashed order.

failure: You spilled the cache twice and called it sorting.
SAVE and LOAD wore name tags. The buffer still needed a map.
delay: SAVE, LOAD, DUR, WIN. It is a tiny menu, not a legal contract.
scorecard: You rebuilt four tiles. The cache is only mildly embarrassed now.

levels.uqiq_moment: The pack raises the buffer load by adding a fourth flashed tile before shuffling the choices.

levels.title: Wait Is First
levels.prompt: Tap the first thing your buffer should do before it guesses.

rules.feedback_on_wrong_tap: The buffer needed a pause, not a button parade.

solution.explanation: The prompt asks what comes before guessing, so WAIT is the correct tap.

failure: TAP sparkled and your finger joined the fan club.
You skipped patience and landed in decorative wrongness.
delay: The correct move is the one that tells the guessing factory to sit down.
scorecard: You tapped WAIT. For one glorious second, impulse lost.

levels.uqiq_moment: A Memory Pack breaks rhythm by rewarding the pause button instead of the active-looking TAP button.

levels.title: Missing Three
levels.prompt: Flash the count. The missing number is bait, not an answer.

solution.explanation: The Level asks for the flashed order, not the missing count value.

failure: You went looking for THREE in a room that never hired it.
The missing number waved from offscreen and your attention followed.
delay: Ignore the absent drama. Rebuild ONE, TWO, FOUR.
scorecard: You remembered the count that actually appeared. THREE can keep sulking.

levels.uqiq_moment: The flashed count skips THREE, but the correct play is to repeat the skip exactly.

levels.title: Second Word Tax
levels.prompt: Type the second word in this fake memory: ALPHA BETA GAMMA.

solution.explanation: The second word in the fake memory list is BETA.

failure: ALPHA walked in first and stole your entire clipboard.
GAMMA stood at the end waving a foam finger. You bought it.
delay: Second means middle here. BETA is trying to look casual.
scorecard: You taxed the second word correctly. BETA has paid in full.

levels.uqiq_moment: A fake memory prompt turns into a direct Text Trap where the middle word is the answer.

levels.title: Desk Cache
levels.prompt: Flash the messy desk, then rebuild the order before it files a complaint.

solution.explanation: Repeat the desk items in the exact flashed order.

failure: You turned a desk into a yard sale with buttons.
The mug was second. Your sequence gave it a new career.
delay: KEY, MUG, PEN, SUN. The desk is messy, not mysterious.
scorecard: You restored the desk cache. The mug stopped judging the pen.

levels.uqiq_moment: Every desk item is ordinary, but the win depends on preserving the exact item order.

levels.title: Status Stack
levels.prompt: Flash the status stack, hide it, then rebuild the stack from top to bottom.

solution.explanation: The stack should be rebuilt in the order it flashed: LOW, MID, HIGH.

failure: HIGH yelled from the button row and you gave it a promotion.
The stack was simple. Your order added office politics.
delay: LOW starts, MID middles, HIGH gets the dramatic top shelf.
scorecard: You rebuilt the status stack. HIGH is no longer cutting the line.

levels.uqiq_moment: The visible choice row starts with HIGH, but the flashed stack starts at LOW.

levels.title: Roast Reorder
levels.prompt: Flash the tiny roast order. Then repeat it without letting YES act helpful.

solution.explanation: YES is only second because that is where it appeared in the flash.

failure: YES smiled first and your finger signed the lease.
You reordered the roast like a menu with trust issues.
delay: NO begins this one. YES can wait its tiny turn.
scorecard: You kept YES in second place. Confidence is coping quietly.

levels.uqiq_moment: The friendliest label is first in the choices but second in the flashed roast order.

levels.title: Full Buffer Final
levels.prompt: Final buffer dump: flash it once, hide it, rebuild the exact order.

solution.explanation: The final Pack 5 sequence is the flashed order from UQIQ through DONE.

failure: The final buffer dumped four tiles and your order wore a fake mustache.
DONE was last in the flash and first in your confidence trap.
delay: UQIQ, DUR, WIN, DONE. The finale came with labels and still wants discipline.
scorecard: You cleared the full buffer. DONE finally earned its parking spot.

levels.uqiq_moment: The final Memory Flash reverses the choice row so DONE appears before it is allowed to be done.
